title: Refactor peer lifecycle to heartbeat-only (remove client-driven delete-peer flow)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Today peer lifetime has two state dimensions:
- `lease_expires_at` — heartbeat-driven, defines "online right now"
- `deleted_at` — explicit `DELETE /peers/:id` from clients, defines "peer ceased to exist"

The explicit-delete flow has been the source of recurring bugs:
- `sync-dmc` (original hard-delete cascade losing audit history — fixed by soft-delete)
- Pi extension calling deletePeer on `session_before_switch` (Pi internal rotation killed peers, fixed by removing handler)
- MCP adapter calling deletePeer on stdin close when borrowing peer_id via SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID env (fixed by borrowed-peer guard)

Each fix has been incremental. The underlying issue is that any client-driven delete is a footgun: clients have many shutdown-like signals (stdin close, session switch, idle teardown, etc.) and tying any of them to peer deletion creates failure modes where the daemon's truth diverges from the process's actual liveness.

## What we want

Collapse the two state dimensions into one: lease expiry. A peer is alive iff its lease is fresh. "Cease to exist" is what happens when nobody heartbeats anymore.

1. Remove deletePeer calls from all normal client code paths:
   - `extensions/pi-synchronize/src/index.ts` teardownProcess
   - `src/mcp/lifecycle.ts` cleanup (currently has borrowed-peer guard; can be removed entirely)
2. Daemon: derive "alive" everywhere from `lease_expires_at > now`
3. `group_members.active` becomes derived (or kept as stored field, but updated by a sweeper instead of by DELETE handler)
4. Alias-reclaim event fires when a new peer claims an alias whose previous holder's lease has expired
5. Keep `DELETE /peers/:id` endpoint as an operator/admin tool only — used to manually evict stuck peers from the web UI; never called by normal client code
6. Background sweeper (optional): periodically marks peers as soft-deleted after lease has been expired for some grace period (e.g. 1 hour) so the peers table doesn't grow unbounded

## Lease tuning

Today DEFAULT_LEASE_MS is 7 days (set high for demo stability). For natural-death detection, this is too long — a crashed Pi process would linger as "online" for a week.

Target: 60–300 seconds. Heartbeat interval (15s) stays the same. Lease is several heartbeats wide so a missed heartbeat doesn't immediately drop the peer.

## Acceptance criteria

- No client code calls deletePeer except in an explicit operator-evict context.
- Pi process exit (kill, OOM, normal shutdown) results in peer dropping from "online" within lease window.
- Group membership shows the peer as inactive (derived from lease expiry).
- Alias-reclaim event still fires when a new peer takes a freed alias.
- Web UI sidebar doesn't flap during normal Pi session rotations.

## Notes

- This deprecates the explicit-delete-on-exit pattern in client code. Skills should not instruct agents to call any delete tool.
- See `sync-dmc` for the original soft-delete work this builds on.
- Tactical patches that should be subsumed by this work: borrowed-peer guard in MCP `cleanup()`, removal of `session_before_switch` handler in Pi extension.

title: Unify MCP and CLI TypeScript repo logic over REST API
description:
MCP and CLI adapters must use the same TypeScript repository/client logic for all synchronize operations, with that shared logic calling the local REST API underneath.

Current implementation:
- src/client.ts owns daemon discovery and low-level requestJson(), so both adapters already share transport/bootstrap.
- src/cli.ts and src/mcp.ts still duplicate operation-level behavior: endpoint paths, request payloads, response interfaces, peer reuse selection, inbox acking, group create/join/leave/send/history, and media share/list/get.
- The duplicated logic can drift, as shown by separate resolveCliRegisterPeerId() and resolveMcpRegisterPeerId() implementations plus repeated requestJson calls across both adapters.

Required outcome:
- Introduce shared TypeScript repo/client operation functions that wrap REST endpoints, e.g. registerPeer, listPeers, sendDm, readInbox, ackInbox, createGroup, joinGroup, leaveGroup, sendGroupMessage, getGroupHistory, listGroups, shareMedia, listMedia, getMedia, getSummary/status as needed.
- Both src/cli.ts and src/mcp.ts should delegate to the shared operation layer instead of building REST paths and JSON bodies directly.
- Keep CLI-specific identity persistence and MCP-specific notification/tool wiring in their adapters, but shared business/API behavior must live in one place.
- Shared types for Peer, Event, Group, Summary, Media, and request/response shapes should be exported from the shared layer and reused by both adapters.
- Preserve the REST API as the underlying boundary; do not bypass src/daemon.ts or duplicate daemon database logic in adapters.

Acceptance criteria:
- grep shows operation-level requestJson calls removed from src/cli.ts and src/mcp.ts except for any deliberate notification polling/bootstrap escape hatch documented in code.
- CLI and MCP registration reuse semantics are consistent and covered by tests.
- Existing tests pass, and coverage is added or updated for at least one shared operation used by both CLI and MCP.
- Public command/tool behavior remains compatible with current command names, tool names, JSON output shape, and error messages unless explicitly changed in a follow-up issue.
close_reason: Implemented shared TypeScript REST operation layer used by both CLI and MCP; added shared API test; typecheck and full test suite pass.

title: Switch web datasource to daemon-owned session
description: What/why/how: update the web DaemonDataSource startup/connect flow to call the daemon local web session endpoint and use the returned peer_id for /web/state, autojoin, group messages, DMs, room refreshes, and optimistic messages. Browser localStorage may remain for UI metadata but must not be the source of group participation identity. Impact area: web/src/data/daemon.ts and browser startup behavior. Whole-plan context: multiple tabs/profiles are controllers for one local human participant, so sending from any surface should render as the same visible 'you'.
acceptance_criteria:
- Multiple browser tabs/profiles converge on the same peer_id from the daemon.
- Web autojoin no longer creates one group member per browser profile.
- Sending group messages and DMs still works from the UI.
- Old localStorage peer IDs do not prevent daemon session identity from becoming source of truth.
notes: How to verify: manual browser test plus web typecheck. Test plan: web typecheck and targeted datasource tests if current setup supports them. Dependencies: sync-z2q.1. Improvements: later replace endpoint consumption with principal/session registration.
close_reason: Switched web DaemonDataSource to POST /web/session as source of participation identity; web build and web typecheck pass after adding missing bun-types dev dependency.

title: Add web-only stale you reclaim behavior
description: What/why/how: when the daemon-owned local web peer joins a group as alias 'you', deactivate/reclaim stale active 'you' memberships held by other peers where tool='web'. Preserve normal alias collision behavior for Claude/Codex/Pi and all other non-web participants. Impact area: group join path, alias collision handling, roster events, inbox fanout, and debugging state. Whole-plan context: this fixes existing broken DB state where an old web peer blocks the new local web session, without allowing duplicate active aliases.
acceptance_criteria:
- Old active web-held 'you' no longer blocks the daemon-owned local web peer.
- Non-web participants holding 'you' still cause alias_collision.
- After reclaim, there is only one active 'you' member for the group.
- Reclaim behavior is observable enough for debugging without weakening alias uniqueness.
notes: How to verify: seed a group with an old web 'you', join with the local web peer, and inspect active members. Test plan: daemon integration tests for web reclaim and non-web collision. Dependencies: sync-z2q.1. Improvements: later principal migration can replace web-specific reclaim with principal-owned membership.
close_reason: Implemented local web session reclaim for stale active web-held alias 'you' while preserving non-web alias collisions; verified with bun test tests/api.test.ts -t 'local web session'.

title: Add daemon local web session endpoint
description: What/why/how: add a daemon endpoint such as POST /web/session that returns a stable daemon-owned local web peer for the current SYNCHRONIZE_HOME. This is the first option-1 implementation slice: browser tabs/profiles must ask the daemon for the local human web participant instead of minting independent group identities. Impact area: daemon REST routing, peer registration/upsert behavior, web API contract, and tests around peer persistence. Whole-plan context: alias uniqueness stays intact; opening /web/ remains a zero-step localhost browser action; option 2 principal identity is deferred.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repeated endpoint calls return the same peer_id for the daemon home.
- Returned peer is registered as tool='web' and visible in web state.
- Default localhost usage requires no extra UI/auth steps beyond existing daemon auth behavior.
- Endpoint shape is documented enough for the web datasource issue to consume.
notes: How to verify: daemon integration test calls the endpoint twice and confirms stable peer identity. Test plan: Bun test around endpoint behavior and peer row persistence. Dependencies: none. Improvements: this can later become a local-principal endpoint when option 2 is implemented.
close_reason: Implemented POST /web/session returning stable daemon-owned local web peer web:local-human; verified with bun test tests/api.test.ts -t 'web session endpoint'.

title: Implement daemon-owned local web session store
description: Goal: ship the immediate option-1 fix so multiple local browser tabs and profiles can use the web UI, join groups as alias 'you', and send messages without alias collisions. Whole-plan context: the daemon becomes the local session store for the human web participant; group alias uniqueness remains intact; browser tabs/profiles are controllers for one daemon-owned local web peer; option 2 principal identity is deferred. Affected areas: daemon web/session API, web DaemonDataSource startup, group membership alias behavior, tests, docs, and synchronize-debugging skill references. Verify by opening /web/ from multiple tabs/profiles, sending messages as one visible 'you', and inspecting one active 'you' group member.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Allow multiple web observers to participate in groups
description: The web UI currently auto-joins every group as alias 'you', so a second browser observer hits alias_collision. We need web observers to remain able to send messages while avoiding collisions between multiple GUI sessions.

title: Pre-refactor: HTTP response snapshot fixture for every daemon endpoint
description:
Capture a golden-test fixture of the daemon's wire contract (status + JSON body shape) for every REST endpoint, recorded against the current pre-refactor daemon. The same fixture must replay byte-identically against the daemon after sync-mkj.10 and sync-mkj.11.

## Why
Integration tests assert specific fields they care about, but no test catches incidental shape drift — a renamed key, a flipped boolean encoding, a dropped field, a reordered array element. The refactor moves ~30 endpoints across ~12 new files. A snapshot fixture is the single strongest safety net for the HTTP wire contract (Layer 1 of the four daemon contracts in the design doc).

## Approach
- New test file tests/daemon-snapshots.test.ts.
- Seed a deterministic daemon state (fixed peer ids, group names, event ids by ordering inserts).
- For every endpoint in src/daemon.ts route(), call it and snapshot {status, normalized JSON body}.
- Normalize volatile fields (timestamps, hostnames, ports, pids, uuids) via a small replacer so the snapshot is stable across runs.
- Cover both happy path and at least one error-path snapshot per endpoint group (404, 403, 409 where applicable).

## In scope
- /health, /status, /summary
- /agent-sessions (register, list, rename, get)
- /peers (register, heartbeat, list, delete)
- /subscriptions
- /dm
- /groups (create, list, get, join, rename, patch, leave, messages, history, media)
- /events/:id, /events/:id/thread
- /media/:id
- /peers/:id/inbox, /peers/:id/inbox/ack, /peers/:id/events
- /web/state (SSE /web/events covered by sync-mkj.12; /web/* static assets snapshot is optional)

## Acceptance
- Fixture captures every endpoint listed above for at least one happy path + one error path.
- Snapshot replay passes on the current branch.
- Snapshot replay must pass unchanged after sync-mkj.10 and sync-mkj.11 land.
- bun run typecheck && bun test green.

## Verification
bun test tests/daemon-snapshots.test.ts

title: Pre-refactor: route precedence + validation + fallthrough regression tests
description:
Backfill test coverage at the seams the daemon split will cross, before any code in src/daemon.ts moves. See docs/daemon-modularization-plan.md for the precedence overlap table and the 5 blind spots.

## In scope
1. **Route-precedence pair assertions** — for each overlap in the design doc's §4 table, add one back-to-back assertion that both endpoints return their expected behavior:
   - GET /agent-sessions vs GET /agent-sessions/:id
   - GET /groups vs GET /groups/:name
   - GET /events/:id vs GET /events/:id/thread
   - PATCH /peers/:id/heartbeat vs DELETE /peers/:id
   - POST /groups vs POST /groups/:name/{join,rename,leave,messages,media}
2. **Validation unit tests** — new tests/daemon-validation.test.ts covering rejected inputs for requireString, optionalString, optionalInteger, optionalObjectJson, optionalNumberArray, requireLocalCallbackUrl, requireGroupName, parseLimit (limit=0, negative, non-numeric), parseCursor (cursor=-1), parseOptionalPositiveInt. These helpers currently only get happy-path coverage through integration tests.
3. **404 fallthrough** — assert unknown path returns {error: {code: 'not_found'}} with status 404.
4. **Subscriber removal on callback failure** — POST /subscriptions, then trigger an event whose callback returns 500 (or where the callback URL refuses); assert ctx.subscribers no longer contains the entry on subsequent attempts.
5. **SSE client cancel cleanup** — open /web/events, cancel the stream client-side, assert ctx.webStateClients does not retain the entry (verified via a subsequent state_changed not throwing on the dead client).

## Why
The current 47 tests assert end-state behavior of each endpoint in isolation, but no test pairs the overlapping endpoints together, no test exercises validation rejections directly, and the subscriber/SSE cleanup branches are not asserted. These are exactly the boundaries sync-mkj.10 and .11 will cross. Without backfill, the split could pass the existing suite while introducing precedence flips or silent cleanup leaks.

## Acceptance
- All 5 new test groups land and pass against the current (pre-refactor) daemon.
- bun test, bun run typecheck pass.
- These tests must continue to pass after sync-mkj.10 and sync-mkj.11.

## Verification
bun run typecheck && bun test

title: Structured error envelope across all tool/HTTP errors
description: Currently rename collisions, alias-taken, peer-not-found, etc. surface as opaque English strings to MCP consumers (e.g. "Alias 'karel' is already active in group 'v0-recheck'."). Bob's #1 friction — needs deterministic error codes to branch, not substring matching. Daemon already returns {error: {code, message}} for HTTP (verified via GET /events/9999), but MCP adapter wraps tool failures as bare strings. Standardize: every MCP tool error returns {code, message, ...details} JSON. Touches src/mcp/util.ts error formatting + every tool.

title: Hard delete of peer cascades to group_members and destroys reclaim/audit trail
description:
`DELETE /peers/:peer_id` removes the peer row. The schema has `group_members.peer_id REFERENCES peers(peer_id) ON DELETE CASCADE`, so every active and inactive group membership for that peer is dropped at the same time. Two downstream invariants break:

1. **Reclaim-audit dead.** Phase 1 of group-policy v0 added `group_member_alias_reclaimed` events fired when a NEW peer takes a freed alias whose previous owner is still present as `active=0`. After a CASCADE delete, the prior owner's row is gone — reclaim cannot fire, and the audit trail is silently lost.

2. **Orphan event rows.** `events.sender_peer_id` has no FK, so events with the deleted peer as sender remain in the table pointing at a non-existent peer. Any join (UI rendering, history export) gets null senders.

## Evidence — observed in live session 2026-05-23

`Manual-Test-V0` group, Pi agent Karen with peer `1c21ac84-076a-4d70-87e4-233c8dcc67eb`:

```
sqlite> SELECT * FROM peers WHERE peer_id='1c21ac84-…';        — 0 rows
sqlite> SELECT * FROM group_members WHERE peer_id='1c21ac84-…'; — 0 rows
sqlite> SELECT event_id, type FROM events WHERE sender_peer_id='1c21ac84-…';
3 group_joined
5 group_message  (thread reply to event 4)
7 group_message  (thread reply to event 6)
9 group_message  (thread reply to event 8)
11 group_message (thread reply to event 8)
13 group_message (thread reply to event 12)
```

The Pi process (pid 15716) was still alive — heartbeats continued to fire and were rejected with `Peer not found` every 15s. The Pi extension teardown never ran (no `teardown begin` line in `~/.synchronize/pi-extension.log`). So an EXTERNAL caller deleted Karen's peer while Pi was still running. The exact caller is unknown — daemon stdout isn't currently logged to a file — but the bug is independent of cause: any hard delete causes the cascade.

## Fix proposals (pick one)

1. **Soft-delete peers.** Add `peers.deleted_at TIMESTAMP NULL`. Treat as deleted everywhere lease/active state is queried. group_members rows remain, supporting reclaim-audit.

2. **Decouple group_members from peers.** Remove the ON DELETE CASCADE. Set `active=0` on member rows when peer is deleted via app-level handler.

3. **Refuse delete while peer holds active memberships.** Force leave-then-delete.

Proposal 1 is preferred — minimal surface change, fixes both the reclaim path AND keeps event sender resolution working.

## Adjacent question

The Pi extension currently has no "re-register on heartbeat-not-found" path. When Karen's peer was deleted underneath her, Pi entered an infinite heartbeat-failure loop instead of recovering. Worth filing as a follow-up after this fix lands.
close_reason: Shipped soft-delete via deleted_at column. Migration v2, DELETE handler does UPDATE not DELETE, all peer reads filter deleted_at IS NULL, ON CONFLICT in upsertPeer clears deleted_at on resurrection. Two lock-in tests in tests/api.test.ts.

title: Validate synchronize state and diagnostics
description:
What/why/how: Add canonical assertions and failure diagnostics for the DM smoke. REST API state is the primary validation surface; SQLite inspection is only a debug aid. AoE state should be checked through AoE CLI JSON first, with internal AoE profile files treated as optional artifacts.

Impact area: Harness assertion/reporting layer. It reads synchronize REST endpoints, may query the synchronize SQLite DB for diagnostics, and archives AoE/tmux outputs on failure.

Dependencies: depends on the CLI DM smoke scenario.
acceptance_criteria:
- REST assertions verify both peers exist, the DM event exists, and the inbox ack state is correct.
- Failure artifacts include harness logs, AoE list/status JSON, synchronize status/peers/events/inbox data, and pane captures.
- SQLite diagnostics are optional and never become the primary pass/fail source.
- Successful runs report a concise summary and clean up by default.
notes: Improvements: Later add HTML or markdown failure reports for easier review, but plain structured logs are enough for this issue.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Drive synchronize CLI DM smoke through panes
description:
What/why/how: Implement the first end-to-end scenario by sending synchronize CLI commands into the AoE/tmux shell panes. The scenario should register two CLI peers, send a direct message from agent 1 to agent 2, and read/ack agent 2 inbox. This proves the harness can exercise real terminal sessions while synchronize remains the system under test.

Impact area: Harness scenario layer and synchronize CLI invocation inside panes. Existing CLI behavior should not be changed unless a bug is discovered and explicitly fixed in a separate implementation issue.

Dependencies: depends on AoE session launch and tmux pane mapping.
acceptance_criteria:
- Scenario starts a fresh temporary SYNCHRONIZE_HOME for synchronize state.
- Agent 1 and agent 2 register through synchronize CLI commands executed inside their panes.
- Agent 1 sends a DM to agent 2 through synchronize CLI.
- Agent 2 reads and acknowledges the inbox through synchronize CLI.
- The harness can parse enough command output or state to correlate the peer IDs and event IDs.
notes: Improvements: Later add group join/leave, mentions, threads, ACL, and respawn scenarios as group-policy v0 stabilizes.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Map AoE sessions to tmux panes
description:
What/why/how: Add libtmux/raw tmux discovery that maps AoE-created shell sessions to stable tmux session/window/pane identifiers. This validates that AoE is exposing normal tmux state and gives the harness the low-level handles needed to send commands and capture output.

Impact area: Harness tmux adapter only. It should prefer stable pane identifiers and avoid relying solely on display names or pane indexes.

Dependencies: depends on AoE shell session launch.
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness discovers every AoE test session through tmux/libtmux.
- Each test agent has a recorded tmux session name/id and pane id.
- Harness captures an initial pane snapshot for every agent.
- Discovery failure writes AoE JSON, tmux list-sessions/list-panes output, and pane captures to the log directory.
notes: Improvements: Later persist these runtime bindings into synchronize agent_sessions metadata. This issue only needs harness-local mapping.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Launch AoE shell sessions in test profile
description:
What/why/how: Teach the harness to create an isolated AoE profile/session set and launch five custom shell sessions for the test agents. These sessions represent terminal-hosted agents but should run plain login shells; the harness will later inject synchronize CLI commands through tmux.

Impact area: Harness orchestration around AoE CLI. The harness should use AoE profile/session naming conventions that avoid colliding with real user sessions and support deterministic cleanup.

Dependencies: depends on the preflight/logging scaffold.
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness can create or select a test AoE profile with a unique name by default.
- Harness launches the requested number of shell sessions with stable names such as sync-agent-1..N.
- AoE list/status JSON is captured after launch.
- Cleanup removes sessions/profile artifacts created by the harness by default.
- `--keep` preserves sessions and prints enough information for manual AoE/tmux inspection.
notes: Improvements: Later support a real Pi-agent launch mode under a separate epic. This issue only covers custom shell sessions.
close_reason: Implemented AoE/tmux fake-shell harness with uv/libtmux preflight, AoE profile/session setup, tmux pane mapping, CLI DM smoke, REST validation, diagnostics, docs, and verified five-agent smoke locally.

title: Add harness preflight and logging scaffold
description:
What/why/how: Add the initial uv-runnable Python harness entrypoint and shared logging/preflight structure. This issue establishes the executable shell for the integration suite without launching AoE sessions yet. It should check required tools, create a run id, create a log directory, and record environment details so later scenario failures are debuggable.

Impact area: New integration harness script/tooling only. It should not alter synchronize daemon, CLI, MCP, or web behavior. It may add lightweight documentation comments or usage output in the script.

Dependencies: none.
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness has a documented command-line interface with flags for profile, agents, keep, verbose, and log-dir.
- Preflight checks for aoe, tmux, bun, uv/python runtime, and libtmux availability.
- Missing dependency errors are actionable and exit before creating AoE or synchronize state.
- A run log directory is created and receives a machine-readable run summary.
notes: Improvements: Later add structured JSON log streaming and richer host diagnostics, but this issue only needs enough logging to support the first smoke test.

title: Build AoE tmux fake-shell integration harness
description:
What is the goal of the epic?
Build a local script-run integration harness for synchronize that uses Agent of Empires as the human/session cockpit, tmux/libtmux as the scripting substrate, and synchronize CLI/REST as the system under test. The first harness validates a minimal direct-message workflow through real terminal sessions without depending on CI or real AI agents.

What are the key features/components/modules/systems/files/logical-components affected by the epic?
Add a uv-runnable Python harness under the repository scripts or integration tooling area. The harness preflights aoe/tmux/bun/uv/python dependencies, starts an isolated AoE profile with five custom shell sessions, maps AoE sessions to tmux panes, sends synchronize CLI commands into panes, validates canonical daemon state via REST, and writes rich diagnostics. Existing daemon/CLI/MCP behavior should remain unchanged unless the harness exposes a real bug.

How to verify / test the implementation of the epic?
Run the harness locally from the new worktree. It should start five AoE shell sessions, register two CLI peers inside panes, send a DM from one peer to another, read/ack the recipient inbox, assert REST state, capture logs, and clean up by default. Existing `bun test` should continue to pass.
design: Production synchronize must not own AoE/tmux lifecycle. This epic is a test harness exception: it owns disposable AoE sessions inside a test profile. peer_id remains the canonical synchronize identity; AoE/tmux identifiers are runtime bindings or diagnostics only.
acceptance_criteria:
- A local developer can run one uv/Python harness command from the worktree.
- The harness fails early with actionable messages when aoe, tmux, bun, uv, python, or libtmux support is missing.
- The harness creates and cleans only its isolated AoE profile/session prefix and temporary SYNCHRONIZE_HOME by default.
- The harness supports a keep/debug mode that preserves sessions and artifacts for investigation.
- A minimal DM smoke scenario passes through real shell panes running synchronize CLI commands.
notes: Full planning context: tmux is the session substrate, AoE is the cockpit/navigation/status surface, raw tmux/libtmux is the low-level automation path, and synchronize is the identity/messaging/workflow plane. The first cut uses shell sessions, not Python fake agents or real Pi agents.
close_reason: Fake-shell AoE/tmux harness epic implemented and verified locally; real Pi-agent integration remains tracked separately by sync-an6.

title: Group policy phase 1: identity & join hardening
description: Make alias bindings on group join identity-aware while preserving respawn-friendly reclaim. Daemon defaults alias to peer.session_name, enforces uniqueness among active=1 members (current partial index stays), and emits a new group_member_alias_reclaimed event whenever a freed alias is claimed by a different peer_id than its previous holder. Roster + history rendering carries a #<peer_id[0:4]> (or #<host_session_id[0:6]>) suffix everywhere. Adds bridge_rename_in_group({ name, new_alias }) scoped to the requesting peer's row. Updates skill docs to reflect that bridge_whoami is the canonical identity source for joins. Closes the rejoin/identity-theft fragility while keeping respawn workflows possible.
design: See session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md sections "Identity & alias" and "Identity flow at join time." Schema: no changes (uses existing group_members rows + new event type). Touchpoints: src/daemon.ts (join handler, new event type), src/api/groups.ts (rename endpoint), src/mcp/tools/groups.ts (new tool), web/src/components/AgentRoster.tsx + TUI render, skills/*.
acceptance_criteria: Two peers with same session_name cannot hold same active alias (409); leave frees the slot; a different peer joining that freed alias emits group_member_alias_reclaimed; roster/history always render suffix; bridge_rename_in_group rejects rename of another peer's row; existing tests in tests/messaging.test.ts and tests/api.test.ts pass with updated assertions.
close_reason: Phase 1 identity & join hardening shipped on worktree-phase1-identity-join-hardening (commits 8b04a11 + follow-up). Delivered: group_member_alias_reclaimed event (different-peer reclaim) + group_member_renamed event; bridge_rename_in_group MCP tool + synchronize group rename CLI; host_session_id on every group_members response row; bridge_join_group description updated; EVENT_TYPES TS const + SQLite CHECK constraint pinning canonical event types (incl. media_changed reserved for future use); skill docs (claude/codex/pi) updated. 24 tests pass; CLI smoke verified (3x register reuses peer). Deferred to follow-ups: sync-dx2 (suffix rendering in TUI/web UI), sync-cg8 (daemon-side identity enforcement, P3). Merge worktree into master when ready.

title: [epic] Group policy v0: identity, threads, mentions, ACL
description: Design and ship the v0 group policy: identity-bound joining with respawn-friendly alias reclaim, Slack-style threads via parent_event_id, mention-gated push notifications, CLI-only admin ACL/blocks, and group description metadata. Full plan in session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md (added in worktree plan-group-policy-v0). Hook-based registration (sync-0ql cluster, shipped in 813b5e3) is the prerequisite identity layer that this epic builds on.

title: Run final modularization verification and cleanup

## Context

description:

title: Merge sync REST-client structure into master without losing MCP fixes
description:
## Problem

There are two active code shapes that need to be reconciled without losing work:

- `master` is the current mainline and contains the installed MCP stdio wrapper fix.
- `sync-6h3-rest-client` is a cleaner branch that introduces a shared REST API facade and unifies the CLI and MCP implementations around it.

The goal is not to blindly merge. The goal is to preserve the best architecture from the sync worktree while retaining master-only fixes and avoiding accidental deletion of project metadata.

## Architectural Difference

Current master shape:

```text
src/client.ts
  | requestJson
  +--> src/cli.ts  direct REST paths and payloads
  +--> src/mcp.ts  direct REST paths and payloads
```

Sync worktree shape:

```text
src/client.ts
  |
  v
src/api.ts       typed REST API facade
  |              status, summary, peers, dm, inbox, events,
  |              groups, media, reusable peer lookup
  +--> src/cli.ts
  +--> src/mcp.ts
```

The sync shape is preferred because REST route strings, payload construction, and response typing are centralized. CLI and MCP become presentation/adaptation layers rather than two independent API clients.

## Preserve From sync-6h3-rest-client

- Add `src/api.ts` as the typed REST facade.
- Keep refactored `src/cli.ts` using `src/api.ts` wrappers.
- Keep refactored `src/mcp.ts` using `src/api.ts` wrappers.
- Keep `tests/api.test.ts` for shared API coverage.
- Keep MCP/CLI parity test updates where they validate the shared API path.
- Keep improved skill guidance around CLI persisted identity and mandatory `--as SESSION_NAME` for group actions.

## Preserve From master

- Keep `bin/synchronize-mcp` as the explicit stdio wrapper:

```ts
import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import { createMcpServer } from "../src/mcp.ts";

const server = createMcpServer();
const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
await server.connect(transport);
```

Reason: master commit `775fa90` fixed installed MCP behavior by avoiding a fragile side-effect-only import wrapper.

- Preserve Claude channel notification payload improvement:
  - `notifications/claude/channel` should emit `params.content` for prompt-visible text.
  - `params.meta` should include structured event metadata: `source`, `event_id`, `type`, sender/recipient/group/media IDs, and `created_at`.
  - MCP server capabilities should advertise `experimental: { "claude/channel": {} }` alongside logging.

- Preserve YAML frontmatter in both skill files.
- Preserve corrected Claude MCP install command format in README:

```bash
claude mcp add --scope user \
  -e SYNCHRONIZE_MCP_MODE=claude \
  -- synchronize bun run /absolute/path/to/synchronize/src/mcp.ts
```

## Do Not Blindly Accept

The sync branch currently deletes these files:

- `.beads/`
- `.claude/settings.json`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- parts of `.gitignore`

These deletions should be reviewed separately. They should not be bundled into the REST-client unification unless the maintainer explicitly wants to remove repo-local project/agent metadata.

## Step-by-Step Merge Plan

1. Commit or stash all current dirty work on `master` before starting integration.
2. Create an integration branch from `master`, for example `merge-sync-rest-client`.
3. Selectively checkout only the structural sync files:
   - `src/api.ts`
   - `src/cli.ts`
   - `src/mcp.ts`
   - `tests/api.test.ts`
   - relevant MCP tests
   - skill files if combining wording carefully
4. Do not checkout metadata deletions or `bin/synchronize-mcp` from sync branch.
5. Reapply the master `bin/synchronize-mcp` explicit stdio wrapper.
6. Reapply the Claude channel notification payload/capability fix to the sync-version `src/mcp.ts`.
7. Combine skill files manually:
   - keep YAML frontmatter
   - keep sync branch identity guidance
   - keep MCP-first guidance where still accurate
8. Update README only if examples drift from current `codex mcp add --help` / `claude mcp add --help` syntax.
9. Run:

```bash
bun run typecheck
bun test
bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts
```

10. Commit in logical chunks:
    - shared REST API facade plus CLI/MCP migration
    - MCP wrapper and Claude channel preservation
    - docs/skills updates
    - optional metadata cleanup as a separate commit only if approved

## Verification Baseline

Read-only validation on `sync-6h3-rest-client` before creating this bead:

- `bun run typecheck`: pass
- `bun test`: 11 pass, 0 fail, 73 expect calls

## Target End State

```text
bin/synchronize-mcp
  |
  v
src/mcp.ts --------------+
  |                      |
  v                      |
src/api.ts <------- src/cli.ts
  |
  v
src/client.ts
  |
  v
daemon REST API
  |
  v
SQLite + media store
```

This keeps the sync worktree's best structural improvement while preserving master-only MCP runtime fixes.
design: Use sync-6h3-rest-client as the structural base for src/api.ts, src/cli.ts, src/mcp.ts, and tests/api.test.ts. Selectively reapply master-only fixes instead of blind merging. Treat metadata deletions (.beads, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json) as an explicit separate decision, not part of the API refactor.
acceptance_criteria: Merged result keeps src/api.ts as the shared typed REST facade for CLI and MCP; preserves master bin/synchronize-mcp explicit stdio wrapper; preserves Claude channel content/meta payload and experimental capability; preserves skill YAML frontmatter and correct MCP install docs; tests and typecheck pass.
notes: Current comparison: sync branch adds src/api.ts and moves CLI/MCP off direct requestJson calls; master has commit 775fa90 fixing installed MCP stdio wrapper; local master analysis also identified a Claude channel notification improvement that emits params.content and params.meta instead of {channel,event}. Sync worktree currently passes bun run typecheck and bun test: 11 pass, 0 fail.
close_reason: Merged master into sync-6h3-rest-client, preserved shared REST API facade plus master MCP wrapper/channel hardening, and verified with typecheck, full bun test, and focused MCP e2e tests.

title: Preserve correct tool attribution for MCP registrations

title: Skill: write reference-v0-plans.md (index of prior plans + handoffs)
description:
## Why this issue exists

This codebase carries substantial historical context: v0 plans, multi-round handoffs, AoE/Pi integration notes, web UI implementation plans, self-evolving operator setup docs, soft-delete shipping handoffs. When a future operator hits a question whose answer lives in one of those documents, they need a single index pointing at all of them. But these documents are large — pulling them into context blindly will burn the conversation budget.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/reference-v0-plans.md` as a pure index file with:

- **TOP-LEVEL DIRECTIVE (mandatory, prominent, first thing in the file)**:
  > Reading any of the documents indexed below WILL consume substantial context. Do NOT load any of them on speculative interest. Only load when:
  > - The user explicitly asks you to read a specific historical plan/handoff, OR
  > - You need a hard reference to a previous implementation session to resolve a current question that cannot be answered from current code state.
  >
  > Default behavior: cite the document and continue working without reading it. Reach for current code first.

- **Index of plans and handoffs**: glob the worktree for plan/handoff/design docs (likely under `docs/`, `bd-related/`, root-level `HANDOFF*.md`, `PLAN*.md`, etc.) and group them by topic:
  - v0 group policy phases
  - soft-delete / peer lifecycle
  - Pi extension + AoE integration
  - Web UI implementation
  - Self-evolving operator setup (rounds 1-3)
  - Daemon discovery + auto-spawn
  - MCP adapter modes
  - Anything else discovered during the glob
- For each entry: relative path, one-sentence purpose, approximate size hint (so the operator can judge context cost), and which current concepts it's the authoritative source for.

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- Top-level directive is the very first content under the heading, formatted as a callout/blockquote so it's hard to miss
- All indexed paths verified to exist at write time
- Each entry is one line — no quotes, no excerpts; the whole point is to NOT bring this content into context
- Cross-references from other skill files (peer-lifecycle.md, etc.) point HERE rather than directly at the historical docs, so the directive always gates access
close_reason: reference-v0-plans.md written with prominent context-cost directive

title: Skill: write glossary.md (codebase navigation reference)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Operators debugging synchronize need to map symptoms to code locations quickly. Without a glossary, every session re-greps the codebase to find 'where does the daemon write daemon.json' or 'where is the borrowed-peer guard'. A curated index of key files, symbols, and concepts collapses that discovery time.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/glossary.md` as a navigation reference covering:

- **Top-level directive**: this file is for fast lookup. For deeper architectural exploration, the agent should read the **serena series memories** (`.serena/memories/architecture-*.md` and related) — those contain narrative architecture context that the glossary intentionally omits.
- **Runtime concepts → code locations**:
  - peer lifecycle → `src/api/peers.ts`, `src/mcp/lifecycle.ts`, `extensions/pi-synchronize/src/index.ts`
  - delivery / push vs inbox → `src/api/inbox.ts`, `src/api/events.ts`, daemon subscriber map in `src/daemon.ts`
  - mention resolution → wherever `MENTION_TOKEN_RE` lives + `group_members.alias` join site
  - thread normalization → daemon's parent_event_id collapse logic
  - heartbeat / lease → `MCP_HEARTBEAT_MS` in `src/constants.ts`, daemon's lease evaluator
  - discovery → `~/.synchronize/daemon.json` writer/reader
  - dev runtime → `SYNCHRONIZE_HOME` env handling, Makefile DEV_SYNC_HOME var
- **Surface map**: REST routes (`src/api/*`), MCP tools (`src/mcp/tools/*`), CLI commands (`src/cli/commands/*`), web UI (`src/web/*`), Pi extension (`extensions/pi-synchronize/*`).
- **Glossary of terms** as used in this codebase: peer, agent_session, group_member, channel, inbox, subscriber, borrowed peer, lease, soft-delete, alias vs session_name, thread root, mention.
- **Env var reference**: all SYNCHRONIZE_* env vars, what reads them, what they affect.
- **Pointers, not narrative**: each entry is one line linking concept → file:line range. Heavy explanation belongs in the per-topic skill files, not here.

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- Top-level directive points to serena series memories for deeper exploration
- All file paths verified to exist in the worktree at write time
- No paragraph-length explanations — this is an index, not a tutorial
close_reason: glossary.md written; points to serena series memories for deeper exploration

title: Makefile: add diagnostic targets (doctor, inspect-peers, inspect-events, inspect-groups)
description:
## Why this issue exists

The synchronize-debugging skill is designed Makefile-first: operators should reach for `make doctor` before writing custom shell pipelines. Today no diagnostic targets exist — every operator session reinvents `sqlite3 ... | grep ...` and `ps -ww` incantations. Putting these behind named targets makes them discoverable, consistent, and skill-referenceable.

## What needs to be done

Add the following targets to the root `Makefile`:

- **`make doctor`** — one-shot diagnostic snapshot:
  - Daemon pid, port, base_url, **worktree** (resolved via `ps -ww -o command=`)
  - Alive peer count / soft-deleted peer count / most-recent updated_at
  - Group count + active-member counts per group
  - Total event count + last 5 events (sender, type, group, parent_event_id, body preview)
  - Tail (last 20 lines) of `pi-extension.log` if present
  - Active tmux sessions matching pattern `sync-*`
  - Output should be plain text, scannable, no fancy formatting.

- **`make inspect-peers`** — peer-focused detail:
  - All peers with: session_name, tool, alive/soft-deleted, online-by-lease, deleted_at if set, agent_session bindings.

- **`make inspect-events N=20`** — last N events with sender alias, type, group, parent_event_id, truncated body.

- **`make inspect-groups`** — groups + active members per group + last activity timestamp.

All targets must:
- Honor `SYNCHRONIZE_HOME` so they work in dev-server mode too.
- Fail gracefully when daemon is not running (print a clear 'daemon not running' message rather than crashing).
- Be added to `.PHONY`.

## Acceptance criteria

- All four targets implemented and exercised manually against a live daemon
- `make doctor` includes worktree-of-daemon prominently (top of output)
- Targets work under `SYNCHRONIZE_HOME=./.dev-synchronize` override
- No new dependencies beyond sqlite3, jq, ps, tail (already in use)

## Notes

This issue is independent of the doc-writing issues and should land FIRST so SKILL.md and detail files can reference `make doctor` confidently. The future full-fledged `synchronize doctor` CLI tool is tracked separately.
close_reason: make doctor + inspect-{daemon,peers,groups,events} added via scripts/doctor.sh; daemon-kill split from clean-slate. Verified against live daemon (read-only) and against non-existent SYNCHRONIZE_HOME (graceful).

title: Skill: write db-queries.md (pure forensic SQL reference)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Forensic SQL is the ultimate truth-source when Makefile targets and CLI output don't cover the question being asked. A curated reference of well-formed queries means operators don't have to relearn the schema each session.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/db-queries.md` as a pure reference file containing copy-pastable SQL for:

- **Live roster**: peers WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND lease_expires_at > datetime('now'), ordered by updated_at DESC.
- **Soft-deleted peers** with when they died and by whom (correlate with pi-extension.log via timestamps).
- **Online derivation**: how lease_expires_at maps to 'online' status.
- **Group members + activity**: group_members join peers with active flag and online derivation.
- **Recent events with thread structure**: events ORDER BY event_id DESC, showing parent_event_id resolution.
- **Inbox depth per peer**: inbox COUNT(*) GROUP BY recipient_peer_id WHERE delivered_at IS NULL.
- **agent_sessions bindings**: host_session_id ↔ peer_id, including stale rows where host_session_id no longer corresponds to an active tmux/Pi session.
- **Mention resolution sanity check**: 'does alias X exist in group Y?'
- **Schema reference**: brief table-level summary (peers, groups, group_members, events, inbox, media, agent_sessions, daemon_state) — column purpose only, NOT full DDL (refer to src/db.ts for canonical).
- **Connection one-liner**: how to open the DB (`sqlite3 $SYNCHRONIZE_HOME/synchronize.db`) and useful pragmas (`.headers on`, `.mode column`).

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- Every query is copy-pastable and includes a comment explaining what it answers
- No long-form prose — this is a reference file
- Schema-summary section refers operators to src/db.ts for canonical DDL rather than duplicating it
close_reason: db-queries.md written

title: Skill: write dev-server-mode.md (isolated SYNCHRONIZE_HOME for ad-hoc testing)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Ad-hoc testing against the live daemon corrupts state the operator's real agents depend on. The dev-server pattern (`SYNCHRONIZE_HOME=$(CURDIR)/.dev-synchronize`) is the safe escape hatch but is not documented anywhere outside the Makefile. Operators need a single reference for 'I want to test this change without touching production state'.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/dev-server-mode.md` covering:

- **Why**: production daemon at ~/.synchronize/ may be backing live agents; touching it loses context and uptime. Dev runtime is fully isolated.
- **Mechanism**: `SYNCHRONIZE_HOME=./.dev-synchronize` env var routes ALL synchronize state (DB, media, daemon.json, logs) to a worktree-local dir.
- **Make-target reference**: `make dev-daemon-kill / dev-daemon-relaunch / dev-clean-slate / dev-reset`. What each does, what each preserves.
- **Launching agents against the dev daemon**: how to start a throwaway Pi agent / Claude session pointing at the dev daemon (env var passthrough through MCP launch).
- **Port-collision avoidance**: dev daemon shares DEFAULT_PORT=58405 with prod; document how to use SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 or a different port when both must coexist.
- **Diagnostics in dev mode**: running `make doctor` against dev runtime via SYNCHRONIZE_HOME override; running `bun run synchronize top` against dev.
- **Recipes**:
  - 'I want to test a daemon change in isolation'
  - 'I want to A/B compare prod vs dev daemon state'
  - 'I want a throwaway round-of-agents demo without polluting prod'
- **Common foot-guns**: env var leaking back into other tools; multiple daemons on conflicting ports; install-* targets pointing MCP clients at the wrong daemon.

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- All dev-* Makefile targets documented
- Port-collision handling explained
- At least three concrete recipes
- 'When you should and shouldn't use dev mode' guidance present

title: Skill: write delivery-forensics.md (DMs, groups, threads, mentions, media)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Delivery is a multi-surface problem (DM, group main channel, thread reply, @mention, media share, agent_session rename) with multiple delivery paths (push to subscribed callback, fallback inbox). Each surface has its own normalization rules and silent-failure modes. Operators need one document that maps a 'message X didn't arrive' symptom to the right surface and the right diagnostic query.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/delivery-forensics.md` covering every delivery surface:

- **DMs**: send response `delivery` field is the truth-source. `pushed_to: []` + `inbox: true` means recipient was not subscribed. How to confirm subscription state.
- **Group main channel**: per-event push targets only mentioned peers + active thread participants; inbox covers all active members. Confirm via events table + group_members.
- **Threads**: 1-level-deep normalization — daemon collapses reply-to-reply to thread root via parent_event_id. Show the SQL to walk a thread.
- **@mentions**: resolved by group_members.alias (case-sensitive exact match), NOT by session_name. Document the `alias_not_in_group` warning and how to fix via bridge_rename_in_group. If this section exceeds ~150 lines, split out to mention-resolution.md.
- **Media**: group-scoped, copied to per-group media dir under SYNCHRONIZE_HOME. How to inspect via filesystem + media table.
- **agent_sessions**: host_session_id ↔ peer_id binding; how renames work (group_member_renamed event); how stale agent_sessions accumulate.
- **Channel vs inbox decision tree**: a small flowchart for 'message sent but not received' diagnosis, leading to the SQL query that confirms.
- **EVENT_TYPES reference**: full list from src/constants.ts and what each implies for downstream delivery.

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- Each delivery surface has its own section with at least one forensic SQL query
- Mention resolution alias-vs-session_name trap is explicitly called out
- Inline split-criterion noted ('split to mention-resolution.md if >150 lines')
- One small decision-tree flowchart for delivery diagnosis

title: Skill: write daemon-forensics.md (worktree provenance, health, restart)
description:
## Why this issue exists

The single highest-leverage diagnostic is 'which worktree is the daemon actually running from?' Without it, operators spend hours wondering why code changes don't take effect. The daemon-spawn-from-master trap (MCP clients auto-spawn a daemon from whichever `synchronize-mcp` binary is symlinked, which may be the wrong worktree) recurs across sessions.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/daemon-forensics.md` covering:

- **Provenance check**: `ps -ww -o command= -p $(jq -r .pid ~/.synchronize/daemon.json)` — does it match your expected worktree? If not, you've hit the daemon-spawn-from-master trap.
- **Recovery**: `make link` from the correct worktree, then `make daemon-kill` so next auto-spawn uses the right binary. Explain why `bun link` rebinds the symlink.
- **Health probes**: `curl /status`, what each field means, what 'unhealthy' typically signals (corrupted DB → wipe; port collision → `lsof -nP -iTCP:58405 -sTCP:LISTEN`).
- **Lock cleanup**: STALE_LOCK_MS=30s; how to manually clear `daemon.lock/` if it gets stuck.
- **Restart cascade**: what `make daemon-kill` / `make daemon-relaunch` / `make clean-slate` each preserve and destroy. Make it crystal clear that `daemon-kill` no longer wipes (post-refactor).
- **Pre-flight checklist** (forward-looking): before launching a round of agents, confirm correct worktree, no stale peers from prior session, ports free, lease settings sane (DEFAULT_LEASE_MS).
- **Wind-down checklist**: how to leave clean state for next session vs. fully reset.
- **Make-target reference table**: every daemon-touching make target with one-line semantics.

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- Daemon-spawn-from-master trap explicitly named and explained
- Make targets are the primary tool surface; raw commands are fallback
- Forward-looking pre-flight and wind-down workflows present
close_reason: daemon-forensics.md written

title: Skill: write peer-lifecycle.md (peer ownership, soft-delete, resurrection)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Peer disappearance is the most common operator failure mode. Diagnosing it requires understanding a non-obvious two-layer ownership model (Pi extension owns; MCP adapter borrows via SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID env var) plus the soft-delete semantics introduced in sync-dmc. Without this file documented, operators re-derive it each session.

## What needs to be done

Create `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/peer-lifecycle.md` covering:

- **Ownership model**: Pi extension owns the peer (loaded inside Pi process); MCP adapter borrows via env var SYNCHRONIZE_PEER_ID. Owner controls delete; borrower must skip delete (see borrowed-peer guard in src/mcp/lifecycle.ts).
- **Lifecycle timeline**: created_at → updated_at (heartbeat) → lease_expires_at → deleted_at. When updated_at and deleted_at collapse to the same instant, DELETE was called — identify who.
- **Pi extension event mapping**: session_start (register or refresh), session_before_switch (must NOT delete; documented absence is intentional), session_shutdown (only legitimate delete path). Reference extensions/pi-synchronize/src/index.ts current implementation.
- **Forensic SQL recipes**: when did peer X die; who is still soft-deleted; who is online (lease > now and deleted_at IS NULL).
- **Resurrection recipe**: re-register with same peer_id; alias rebind via bridge_rename_in_group; subscriber re-attach via process restart (resurrection alone does NOT restore subscriber state — note this as the 'alive but unreachable' trap).
- **Heartbeat-only future**: forward-reference sync-6mz for the architectural cleanup that removes client-driven delete entirely.
- **Common failure signatures**: 'Peer not found' 404 spam in pi-extension.log, deleted_at set while process is still running, agent appearing online but receiving no channel pushes.

## Acceptance criteria

- File exists at the path above
- Covers both ownership layers explicitly
- Documents the 'alive but unreachable' state and how to detect it
- Forensic SQL examples are copy-pastable
- References to other docs use skill names, not `@` syntax
close_reason: peer-lifecycle.md written

title: Create synchronize-debugging skill (operator debugging reference)
description:
## Why this issue exists

Across multiple debugging sessions on this codebase, the same diagnostic flows recur: identifying which worktree the daemon is running from, distinguishing peer soft-delete from peer-not-subscribed, working out why an @-mention didn't push, tracing thread normalization, deciding between wiping state and preserving it, and standing up isolated dev runtimes for ad-hoc testing.

Currently each operator session rediscovers these flows from scratch, often re-tripping known traps (daemon-spawn-from-master, MCP-adapter borrowed-peer delete, alias-vs-session_name mention mismatch). A versioned, in-repo skill at `.claude/skills/synchronize-debugging/` makes that institutional knowledge discoverable and reusable.

## What needs to be done

Create a progressive-discovery skill with a tight top-level jumppad and detail files loaded on-demand:

- SKILL.md — anatomy snapshot, orientation checklist (Makefile-first), symptom router

The skill must be generalized — usable for future diagnostic and debugging problems we haven't hit yet, not just the bugs encountered in 2026-05-23 sessions. Makefile is the primary tool surface; raw `sqlite3`/`ps` commands are documented as fallbacks.

title: Thread summaries via LLM (Vercel AI SDK + OpenRouter)
description:
Add LLM-generated thread summaries for cold, non-trivial threads, computed once
in the daemon and exposed via REST, CLI, and MCP. Web UI integration is a
deliberate follow-up.

# Goal

Agents and humans should be able to ask "what happened in this thread?" and get
a 2-4 sentence summary without re-reading the whole transcript. Compute happens
once per thread (last-write-wins cache) and is invalidated when the thread
grows or the prompt template changes.

# Scope (v0, this branch)

In:
- daemon-internal summarization worker + summary read/write paths
- REST: GET /threads/:root_event_id/summary, POST /threads/:root_event_id/summary
- CLI: synchronize threads summary ROOT_ID [--refresh] [--strategy ...] [--k N] [--first-k N] [--last-k N]
- MCP: bridge_get_thread_summary (separate tool; do NOT inline into bridge_get_thread)
- new SQLite table thread_summaries
- discoverable_threads view tweak to expose last_event_id (MAX(event_id) in thread)
- tests using Vercel AI SDK MockLanguageModelV1 (no real LLM call in default suite)

Out (tracked elsewhere or later):
- Web UI rendering of summaries (deferred follow-up; backend must return cleanly
  when feature is disabled so web can show "no summaries available")
- per-group opt-in flag (groups.summaries_enabled) - future tightening
- live update events / SSE notification for web - future
- rate-limiting forced regen - future
- multi-row cache keyed by strategy - explicitly rejected (single row, LWW)
- inline summary in bridge_get_thread payload - explicitly rejected

# Worktree + branching

- Worktree: create a new worktree at
  /Users/abhirupdas/Codes/Personal/synchronize-worktrees/feature-thread-summaries
- Base branch: feature/sql-event-query-surfaces (NOT master). This feature
  depends on the SQL/thread surfaces (transcript route, discoverable_threads
  view, thread API facade) that branch ships.
- Final branch name: feature/thread-summaries
- Rebase onto master after sql-event-query-surfaces merges; do not target master
  directly until then.

# Library + provider

- LLM library: Vercel AI SDK (ai + provider packages). TS-native, Bun-friendly,
  swap providers via one import.
- Default provider: OpenRouter (@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider).
- Default model: anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5.
- Config envs:
  - OPENROUTER_API_KEY: presence of this key is the master enable switch.
    No key -> worker idle, REST/CLI/MCP read endpoints return
    {summary: null, status: "disabled"}, force-regen endpoints return 503.
  - SYNCHRONIZE_LLM_PROVIDER (default "openrouter")
  - SYNCHRONIZE_LLM_MODEL (default "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5")
- Stored model column format on each row: "openrouter:anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5"
  so provenance survives provider/model changes.

# Worker behavior

- Single setInterval loop inside the daemon process; lifecycle tied to existing
  daemon start/stop.
- Poll interval: SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, default 15 * 60 * 1000
  (15 min). Env-adjustable down to seconds for testing (10-20s during dev).
- Cold-thread gate: SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_COLD_AFTER_MS, default 30 * 60 * 1000
  (30 min). Only summarize threads where last_activity_at <= now - cold_after.
- Min replies gate: SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_MIN_REPLIES, default 3. Worker skips
  threads with fewer replies. Force-regen ignores this gate.
- Batch up to 10 stale-eligible threads per tick. Serialize the LLM calls
  (one at a time) to keep memory + provider pressure bounded.
- In-memory Set<rootEventId> of in-flight summarizations prevents double work
  across overlapping ticks.
- Failure handling: try once, log the error, store in an in-memory map
  {rootEventId -> lastErrorAt}, back that thread off for 30 min, continue. No
  DB column for errors in v0.
- Worker is best-effort and must never block message delivery, group ops, or
  daemon shutdown.

# Eligibility predicate (worker)

A thread is summarizable when ALL of:
1. It appears in discoverable_threads (root group_message with >=1 reply)
2. reply_count >= SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_MIN_REPLIES (default 3)
3. now() - last_activity_at >= SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_COLD_AFTER_MS (default 30m)
4. Either no row in thread_summaries OR
   (existing.covered_last_event_id < discoverable_threads.last_event_id) OR
   (existing.prompt_version < CURRENT_PROMPT_VERSION)
5. Not in the in-memory in-flight set
6. Not currently in error backoff window

# Strategies

Strategies are how the worker (or caller) picks which messages to feed the LLM.
Root is ALWAYS included; K parameters count REPLIES, not total events.

Supported:
- all                  -- root + every reply
- first_k(k)           -- root + first k replies (default k=3)
- last_k(k)            -- root + last k replies
- first_last(first_k, last_k) -- root + first first_k replies + last last_k replies (deduped if overlap)

Worker default strategy: first_k with k=3 (root + first 3 replies = 4 messages
total). Configurable via:
- SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_STRATEGY (default "first_k")
- SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_K (default 3)
- SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_FIRST_K (default 5, used when strategy=first_last)
- SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_LAST_K (default 10, used when strategy=first_last)

Hard input cap for `all` strategy: if rendered transcript exceeds ~50KB, fall
back to first_last(5, 10) for that call. Prevents pathological cost on huge
threads.

# Caching semantics

One row per thread (PRIMARY KEY = root_event_id). Last write wins. Strategy +
params are metadata on the row, not part of the key. Forced regen with a
different strategy OVERWRITES the existing row -- there is intentionally no
multi-strategy history.

# Schema

New table:

  CREATE TABLE thread_summaries (
    root_event_id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES events(event_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    summary                 TEXT    NOT NULL,
    model                   TEXT    NOT NULL,                  -- e.g. "openrouter:anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5"
    strategy                TEXT    NOT NULL,                  -- "all" | "first_k" | "last_k" | "first_last"
    strategy_params_json    TEXT    NOT NULL,                  -- e.g. {"k":3} or {"first_k":5,"last_k":10}
    prompt_version          INTEGER NOT NULL,
    covered_last_event_id   INTEGER NOT NULL,
    covered_event_count     INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at              TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ','now')),
    updated_at              TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ','now'))
  );

View tweak (so staleness can be detected by event id, not just timestamp):

  -- discoverable_threads must additionally expose:
  --   MAX(reply.event_id, root.event_id) AS last_event_id

Optional new view for ergonomics:

  CREATE VIEW thread_summary_status AS
    SELECT
      dt.root_event_id,
      dt.group_name,
      dt.last_activity_at,
      dt.last_event_id,
      dt.reply_count,
      ts.summary,
      ts.model,
      ts.strategy,
      ts.strategy_params_json,
      ts.prompt_version,
      ts.covered_last_event_id,
      ts.updated_at,
      CASE
        WHEN ts.root_event_id IS NULL THEN 1
        WHEN ts.covered_last_event_id < dt.last_event_id THEN 1
        ELSE 0
      END AS stale
    FROM discoverable_threads dt
    LEFT JOIN thread_summaries ts ON ts.root_event_id = dt.root_event_id;

# REST surface

GET /threads/:root_event_id/summary
  Response (200):
    {
      summary: string | null,
      model: string | null,
      strategy: string | null,
      strategy_params: object | null,
      prompt_version: number | null,
      covered_last_event_id: number | null,
      updated_at: string | null,
      stale: boolean,
      status: "ready" | "pending" | "disabled"
    }
  - status="disabled" when no OPENROUTER_API_KEY.
  - status="pending" when feature enabled but no row yet.
  - status="ready" when a row exists (stale flag indicates if it's behind).

POST /threads/:root_event_id/summary
  Body (all optional):
    {
      strategy?: "all" | "first_k" | "last_k" | "first_last",
      k?: number,
      first_k?: number,
      last_k?: number
    }
  - Force regen, ignores cold gate and min-replies gate.
  - 503 when feature disabled.
  - 200 with the same shape as GET, freshly written.
  - Synchronous: blocks the caller until the LLM call returns. (Async/job
    queue is future work.)

# CLI surface

synchronize threads summary ROOT_ID
  -- Reads cached summary. Format: text by default; --format json for raw.

synchronize threads summary ROOT_ID --refresh
  -- Force regen via POST.

Flags (when --refresh): --strategy, --k, --first-k, --last-k.

Add command file: src/cli/commands/threads.ts (extend the existing threads
command added in this branch). Update src/cli/help.ts.

# MCP surface

New tool: bridge_get_thread_summary

  Input:
    {
      root_event_id: number,
      force?: boolean,                                    // default false
      strategy?: "all" | "first_k" | "last_k" | "first_last",
      k?: number, first_k?: number, last_k?: number
    }
  Output:
    Same shape as GET /threads/:id/summary response.

Tool description must tell agents:
- Prefer cached read (force=false); only set force=true when the cache is stale
  or strategy needs to change.
- Summary is computed for cold threads only by the worker; forced regen is
  always allowed.
- Returns status="disabled" when the daemon has no LLM provider configured.

Do NOT extend bridge_get_thread's payload to embed the summary. Keep tools
single-purpose.

# Prompt template

System:
  You are summarizing a chat thread between local agents.
  Output 2-4 sentences covering: what was discussed, who participated, and any
  decision or open question. Do not include preamble, headings, or quotes.

User content:
  The deterministic transcript already produced by GET /threads/:id?format=transcript
  on the SQL branch. Reuse it; do not reinvent.

Prompt version starts at 1. Bump in code whenever the system prompt changes;
the worker treats older-version rows as stale automatically.

# Failure modes

- Missing API key: feature disabled; endpoints behave per status="disabled".
- LLM call error: log + 30-min per-thread in-memory backoff; worker moves on.
- LLM returns empty/whitespace: treat as failure, do not write a row, apply
  backoff.
- Transcript > 50KB on "all" strategy: silently fall back to first_last(5,10).
- daemon shutdown mid-summarize: row simply not written; worker re-attempts on
  next tick after restart.

# Testing

- Unit-style tests using Vercel AI SDK's MockLanguageModelV1 so the suite stays
  offline and deterministic.
- Cover:
  - worker picks only cold + >=min_replies + stale threads
  - in-flight set prevents double summarization
  - force-regen bypasses cold + min-replies
  - strategy selection produces the expected slice of events
  - LWW caching (second write overwrites first)
  - prompt_version bump invalidates cache
  - discoverable_threads.last_event_id is correct (root-only and with replies)
  - REST GET returns "disabled" with no key
  - REST POST 503s with no key
  - MCP tool returns the same shape as REST
  - CLI table/json output formats
- Run with SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 to avoid the known default-port collision
  (sync-anr).
- Real-provider smoke test gated behind an env flag (SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_LIVE_TEST=1),
  NOT in default suite.

# Implementation outline (single PR, single branch)

1. Add Vercel AI SDK deps:
     bun add ai @openrouter/ai-sdk-provider
2. Schema migration in src/db.ts:
   - new table thread_summaries
   - update discoverable_threads view to expose last_event_id
   - optional view thread_summary_status
3. New module src/llm/ with provider factory + prompt template + version constant.
4. New module src/summarize/ with:
   - selectThreadEvents(rootEventId, strategy, params) -> Event[]
   - renderTranscript(events) -- reuse the existing transcript renderer
   - summarizeThread(rootEventId, opts) -> row (does the LLM call + upsert)
   - workerTick() -> picks eligible threads and runs summarizeThread for each
5. Wire worker lifecycle into daemon.ts start/stop.
6. New API routes in src/api/threads.ts (extend existing file):
   - GET  /threads/:root_event_id/summary
   - POST /threads/:root_event_id/summary
7. CLI: extend src/cli/commands/threads.ts and src/cli/help.ts.
8. MCP: new tool file src/mcp/tools/summary.ts; register in src/mcp/server.ts.
9. Tests under tests/ (api, worker, mcp-e2e additions).
10. README + agent skill stop-gap mentions (full progressive-skill update remains
    blocked on sync-b8p, mirroring how sync-s7r handled it).

# Non-goals / explicit rejections

- No multi-strategy cache rows. One row per thread.
- No inline summary inside bridge_get_thread response.
- No async job queue / status polling. POST is synchronous in v0.
- No retry beyond the 30-min in-memory backoff.
- No per-group enable flag in v0.
- No web UI changes in this branch.
- No DB column for last_error_at in v0 (in-memory only).
- No SSE/event emission for summary updates in v0.

# Acceptance criteria

- bun run typecheck passes.
- SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 bun test passes (full suite + new tests).
- With OPENROUTER_API_KEY set, manually triggering a cold thread via
  POST /threads/:id/summary produces a non-empty summary and writes a
  thread_summaries row.
- Without OPENROUTER_API_KEY:
  - daemon starts cleanly
  - GET returns status="disabled"
  - POST returns 503
  - worker does not tick
- Setting SYNCHRONIZE_SUMMARY_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=10000 in dev makes the worker
  visibly run every 10s and pick up newly-cold threads.

title: Parallel test runs collide on DEFAULT_PORT=58405 — tests that spawn daemon via CLI/MCP subprocess need port=0 propagation
description:
The 2026-05-23 port-pin (DEFAULT_PORT=58405, commit b58e0e0) broke parallel test execution. Tests that spawn daemons via CLI (`bun run synchronize <cmd>`) or via the MCP stdio adapter (`bun run src/mcp.ts`) do not always propagate SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 down through the spawned subprocesses, so multiple concurrently-running tests all try to bind to the same default port.

## Symptom

`bun test` (parallel default) shows 5000ms timeouts on:
- tests/messaging.test.ts: groups/durable-restart, media-share, Claude-hook-env-gated
- tests/mcp.test.ts: Codex NotificationBridge
- tests/health.test.ts: CLI-launched daemon stays alive
- extensions/pi-synchronize/tests/subscription.test.ts: PiEventSubscription DM push

All pass in isolation. All fail under parallel.

## Workaround applied

- Added SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 to spawn envs in tests/api.test.ts, tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts, tests/messaging.test.ts where direct spawns were missing it.
- Some sites still don't propagate cleanly (CLI subprocesses spawning further subprocesses).

## Proper fix options

1. **In-process daemon for tests.** Same idea as the broader test-speed bd issue (sync-XXX, just filed) — refactor daemon startup to be embeddable in-process. Eliminates port-binding entirely.

2. **Random-port-by-default again.** Revert DEFAULT_PORT to 0. Reintroduce the Pi extension's reconnect issue but solve it the other way (retry-with-rediscover in client.ts).

3. **Test-helper that always propagates port=0.** Wrap Bun.spawn / Bun.spawnSync with a helper that injects SYNCHRONIZE_PORT=0 plus a unique SYNCHRONIZE_HOME, used uniformly across tests. Mechanical refactor.

Acceptance: `bun test` runs cleanly with 0 failures and 0 timeouts in <30s.

Fix: rewrite the description to lead with the group-scoped use case ('Returns the active alias/peer_id roster for a group, or all peers when group is omitted'), and ensure the response includes alias + active + joined_at when group-scoped. Keeps MCP tool count flat. Especially valuable for small-model agents — fewer tools = better targeting.

title: Add real Pi three-agent group baton workflow
description:
What/why/how:
Add a real Pi AoE/tmux integration scenario where three Pi agents coordinate through a synchronize group in a pass-the-baton workflow. Agent 1 creates a JSON baton payload and sends it to Agent 2. Agent 2 reads it, modifies it, and sends it to Agent 3. Agent 3 modifies it again and sends it back to Agent 1. Agent 1 validates the final state against the expected transformation and confirms the loop completed.

Impact area:
- scripts/integration-aoe/sync_itest_aoe/scenarios/ real Pi group-policy or new baton scenario.
- scripts/integration_group_policy_pi.py or a new wrapper if the workflow should stay separate.
- Existing Pi warmup, MCP self-discovery, group history, thread/message validation, loop guards, and diagnostics.

Acceptance criteria:
- Scenario launches three real Pi agents through AoE/tmux using the unified temporary Pi home.
- Agents use synchronize MCP group tools only; no shell or CLI fallback.
- Prompts remain self-discovery based and do not spoon-feed the agent its own peer_id/session id.
- REST state verifies the baton messages, sender sequence, final payload content, and group aliases.
- Transcript evidence includes the expected MCP group tool calls for each participating agent.
- --keep preserves AoE profile, tmux sessions, and run state for manual inspection.

How to verify:
- Run the real Pi baton workflow locally with generous timeouts.
- Inspect group history JSON, pane captures, and Pi transcripts.

Test plan:
- Manual/local e2e smoke only; deterministic fake-shell group-policy coverage remains the stable regression layer.
close_reason: Implemented thread-baton real Pi AoE/tmux workflow with transcript-backed no-history assertion; verified with TTHREAD08 --keep smoke run.
