title: Modularize integration harness support code
description:
## Goal
Refactor the integration harness code so the suite can grow beyond the current shell smoke and real Pi DM smoke without accumulating large copy-pasted scripts. The current shape works functionally, but `scripts/integration_tmux.py` and `scripts/integration_pi.py` both carry overlapping responsibilities: run metadata, command execution/logging, preflight checks, AoE profile/session lifecycle, tmux pane discovery/capture, REST diagnostics, cleanup, and scenario orchestration. Future workflows such as group join/send/history, inbound Pi push, media share, blocked-agent detection, and multi-agent workflows should be able to reuse a small set of primitives rather than duplicating full harness classes.

## High-level refactor direction
Create a small Python integration support package under `scripts/integration/` or similar, with thin executable entrypoints for each scenario. Keep the user-facing commands stable (`uv run scripts/integration_tmux.py`, `uv run scripts/integration_pi.py`) by turning those files into wrappers or by preserving equivalent scripts that import the shared modules. The architecture should separate reusable infrastructure from workflow scenarios.

## Proposed module boundaries
- `runtime.py`: run ids, repo discovery, state/log directory layout, JSON/text log writing, command execution with stdout/stderr capture, common `HarnessError`, cleanup helpers, and environment construction.
- `aoe.py`: AoE profile creation/deletion, session add/start/remove, AoE list/status collection, profile naming helpers, and current-run AoE session id tracking.
- `tmux.py`: libtmux availability checks, pane discovery, pane mapping by AoE session id prefix, pane capture, literal prompt/command sending, and the Pi-specific named `Enter` submission helper. This module should encode the learned rule that Pi TUI prompt submission needs `tmux send-keys ... Enter`, not `C-m`.
- `sync_rest.py`: daemon startup through `bun run src/cli.ts status`, discovery loading, REST JSON helpers, `/peers`, `/events`, `/agent-sessions`, and diagnostic snapshots.
- `pi_env.py`: isolated Pi home provisioning, copied `auth.json`, minimal `settings.json`, worktree-local `mcp.json`, worktree extension/skill path validation, Pi command construction, and session/transcript discovery. It must preserve the invariant that tests never mutate real `~/.pi/agent`.
- `scenarios/cli_dm.py`: deterministic shell/CLI DM smoke currently in `integration_tmux.py`.
- `scenarios/pi_mcp_dm.py`: real Pi MCP DM workflow currently in `integration_pi.py`, including auto-registration, MCP-ready selection, `bridge_whoami` + `bridge_list_peers` + `bridge_dm` prompt, REST event assertion, and transcript evidence.

## Low-level cleanup points
- Deduplicate `AgentPane`, `HarnessError`, `command`, `write_text`, `write_json`, `http_json`, `capture_all_panes`, `aoe_list`, `collect_aoe_state`, and daemon cleanup logic.
- Remove broad monolithic harness classes where possible; each scenario should read as setup -> action -> assertions -> diagnostics.
- Make current-run AoE/tmux mapping robust by always preferring AoE session id prefixes over title-based matching, so old kept sessions do not contaminate new runs.
- Keep CLI shell command submission and Pi TUI prompt submission as separate primitives. Shell commands may use command markers; Pi prompts must use literal text plus named `Enter`.
- Keep identity discipline explicit: harness REST state may use peer ids for assertions, but prompts to real agents should stay high-level unless a scenario specifically tests low-level identity handling. Normal Pi prompts should make the agent discover itself with `bridge_whoami` and discover peers/groups with MCP tools.
- Keep generated runtime state under ignored `.synchronize-itest/` by default, with per-run subdirectories.
- Ensure `--keep` behavior remains consistent: preserve AoE/tmux/Pi/synchronize runtime state for inspection; default cleanup removes live sessions and daemon state while keeping useful logs where appropriate.
- Preserve manual/local nature of real Pi tests; they should remain outside `bun test` and CI.

## Compatibility requirements
- Existing command lines should continue to work, especially:
  - `uv run scripts/integration_tmux.py`
  - `uv run scripts/integration_tmux.py --keep`
  - `uv run scripts/integration_pi.py`
  - `uv run scripts/integration_pi.py --keep`
- Keep inline `uv` script dependency handling for executable wrappers, or document the replacement if a package layout requires it.
- Do not change the behavior or scope of the already-verified shell CLI smoke and real Pi MCP DM smoke during the refactor except to preserve behavior through shared modules.

## Verification
- `python3 -m py_compile` over all integration Python modules and wrappers.
- `uv run scripts/integration_tmux.py --help` and `uv run scripts/integration_pi.py --help`.
- Full deterministic shell smoke: `uv run scripts/integration_tmux.py`.
- Real Pi smoke, manual/local: `uv run scripts/integration_pi.py`, plus one `--keep` run during development to inspect AoE/tmux state.
- Project checks: `bun run typecheck` and `bun test`.

## Non-goals
- Do not add CI execution for real Pi workflows.
- Do not broaden scenarios to groups/media/inbound push as part of this refactor. The point is to create the structure that makes those future scenarios straightforward.
- Do not mutate global Pi config or rely on production `~/.pi/agent` beyond copying `auth.json` into the isolated run directory.
design: The refactor should make integration infrastructure reusable before adding more workflows. Prefer small Python modules with explicit responsibilities over a large inheritance hierarchy. The wrappers should stay boring and stable; scenario modules should contain workflow-specific prompts and assertions; shared modules should contain all AoE/tmux/Pi/synchronize runtime mechanics. This preserves the current successful behavior while making future Beads issues for complex workflows independently implementable.
acceptance_criteria:
- Detailed module split exists for reusable integration runtime, AoE, tmux, REST, Pi environment, and scenarios.
- Current shell CLI smoke and real Pi MCP DM smoke still pass with the same public commands.
- Real Pi prompt remains high-level and does not pass recipient peer ids for normal discovery workflows.
- The Pi named-Enter tmux caveat is encoded in a shared helper and documented.
- Future scenarios can be added without duplicating AoE/tmux/Pi provisioning code.
close_reason: Implemented scripts/integration-aoe reusable AoE/tmux integration support modules, thin stable wrappers, CLI DM and Pi MCP DM scenarios, docs, and verification: py_compile, wrapper --help, deterministic tmux smoke, real Pi smoke, bun typecheck, bun test.

title: Validate Pi auto-registration and MCP DM smoke
description: Add the first real Pi scenario: verify extension-driven Pi peer and agent-session registration, prompt one Pi agent to use synchronize MCP tools to send a DM to another Pi peer, and assert REST state plus Pi transcript evidence.

title: Launch real Pi sessions through AoE tmux harness
description: Extend the integration harness so it can create an isolated AoE profile, launch real interactive Pi sessions, map them to tmux panes, and capture diagnostics using the same cockpit/tmux approach as the fake-shell harness.

title: Provision isolated per-worktree Pi test environment
description: Create the worktree-local Pi harness state layout and provision a minimal temporary Pi home from the current worktree. The setup should copy only auth.json from the user's real Pi config, write minimal settings and MCP config, and load the worktree's Pi extension and skill without mutating ~/.pi/agent.

title: Daemon-side identity enforcement for self-scoped mutations
description: The daemon currently trusts peer_id from request bodies for self-scoped operations (group rename, group create's creator_peer_id, etc). Adapter layers enforce that callers only pass their own peer_id, but a direct REST caller could mutate a different peer's row. v0 explicitly accepts this trust model (local-single-machine, hostile-direct-REST is out of scope). File this as a follow-up so we don't lose the design choice. Options for v1 if/when needed: (a) require a bearer/peer-scoped token signed by the daemon on register; (b) move identity guardrails into the daemon, breaking the current 'daemon trusts callers' invariant; (c) hash-chain commit log. Pick one when there's a concrete threat model.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded (memo or ADR) about whether to add daemon-side identity enforcement for self-scoped writes; if yes, implement; if no, document the trust boundary explicitly in CLAUDE.md / docs/adr/. Either way close with a written rationale.

title: Add real Pi-agent AoE integration smoke
description:
What is the goal of the epic?
Add a later, optional real-agent integration smoke that launches real Pi coding-agent sessions through AoE and uses the same synchronize identity/messaging/workflow concepts as the fake shell harness. This epic exists to track the future production-like path after the deterministic shell harness is working.

What are the key features/components/modules/systems/files/logical-components affected by the epic?
Future work will extend the AoE/tmux harness with a real Pi launch mode, Pi setup/auth preflight, Pi-specific synchronization registration, and scenarios for DMs, groups, threads, mentions, and ACL once those features stabilize. No implementation details are specified yet; this epic is intentionally a placeholder for the real-agent phase.

How to verify / test the implementation of the epic?
Future verification should prove that real Pi sessions launched through AoE can register with synchronize, communicate through synchronize, and remain inspectable through AoE/tmux. Exact scenarios should be planned after the fake-shell harness and group-policy v0 work are stable.
design: This epic is intentionally deferred. It should not block the fake-shell scaffold. It exists to keep the roadmap visible without forcing premature choices about Pi auth, command shape, or scenario coverage.
acceptance_criteria:
- Epic captures the future real Pi-agent integration objective separately from the fake-shell harness.
- No child implementation tasks are required until the first harness version is complete.
- Future work should reuse the harness primitives from the AoE/tmux fake-shell epic where possible.

title: Group policy phase 3d: group description metadata
description: Add a nullable groups.description TEXT column, settable at create and mutable via the privileged CLI (synchronize group describe <group> <text>). Surfaces in listGroups responses and the web chat UI roster/header so agents can discover group purpose without out-of-band coordination. Trivially small phase -- intentionally separate so it can land in any order with the other phase 3 work.
design: See session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md "Group metadata" section. Schema: ALTER TABLE groups ADD COLUMN description. API: POST /groups accepts optional description; PATCH /groups/:name accepts { description? } (CLI-only consumer). Touchpoints: src/db.ts, src/api/groups.ts, src/api/types.ts (Group type), src/daemon.ts, src/cli/commands/group.ts, web/src/components/RoomHeader.tsx or Sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: POST /groups with description persists and returns it; listGroups includes description in Group rows; PATCH /groups/:name with { description } updates; CLI command sets description; web UI displays it where the design calls for a topic line.
close_reason: Phase 3d shipped: groups.description schema + POST/PATCH + describe CLI + RoomHeader topic line. Tests 33 pass / 0 fail.

title: Group policy phase 3c: ACL / blocks (CLI-only admin)
description: Add per-peer / per-host-session access control to groups via a new group_acl table. v0 stores blocked status only (allowed is reserved for v1 invite-list flows). Key types: peer_id and host_session_id -- the latter lets admins block a specific Claude/Pi session even after it respawns under a new peer_id. Block effect: group remains visible in listGroups (no listing filter), but join/send/share_media/history/rename_in_group return 403 group_forbidden. All ACL writes go through privileged CLI on the daemon host -- no MCP tools touch ACL. New CLI commands: synchronize group block/allow/acl. Defaults remain wide open (no rows -> no blocks).
design: See session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md sections "Access control" and "ACL enforcement chokepoint." Schema: new group_acl table with PK (group_id, key_type, key_value) and idx_group_acl_key (key_type, key_value). API: GET/POST /groups/:name/acl, DELETE /groups/:name/acl/:key_type/:key_value. Daemon-side ensureNotBlocked(group_id, peer_id) chokepoint called from all group ops. CLI: synchronize group block/allow/acl/describe under src/cli/commands/group.ts.
acceptance_criteria: Blocked peer (by peer_id) sees the group in listGroups but gets 403 on join/send/history/rename_in_group; blocked host_session_id key blocks every peer with that binding; CLI commands round-trip create/list/delete entries; ensureNotBlocked never permits an op when an ACL row matches by either key type.
notes: Deferred from v0 as a deliberate scoping decision (2026-05-23). Rationale: no adversary on a single-machine single-user tool; daemon-side identity enforcement (sync-cg8) is also deferred, so policing group access without policing identity is incoherent; plan already deferred everything around 'blocked' (role, visibility, allowed, per-thread ACL) to v1. Design intact in session-tracker/plan-group-policy-v0.md. Reconsider when an actual use case appears: untrusted/autonomous agents, multi-user daemon, or sensitive groups other agents shouldn't peek into.

title: Baseline: a11y + bundle-size measurements before migration
description:
## Why
We can't claim "shadcn / Radix gave us accessibility wins" without measuring before and after. Same for bundle size — Radix primitives are 5-15 kB gzipped each; we should know the delta.

## Acceptance criteria

### Accessibility baseline (today)
- [ ] Run axe-core (or Lighthouse a11y audit) against the current preview at http://127.0.0.1:47823/web/ in both light and dark mode.
- [ ] Manually test keyboard nav on each of the four context-menu surfaces (Sidebar room item, MessageRow, AgentRoster card, future TimelineRail node). Document which key events fail.
- [ ] Manually test mention popup with keyboard only — can a screen-reader user open, navigate, select? Document failures.
- [ ] Screen-reader smoke test with VoiceOver: are messages announced? Author? Mentions? Threads? Document gaps.
- [ ] Save findings to docs/a11y-baseline.md.

### Bundle baseline (today)
- [ ] Record current web/dist/main.*.js gzipped size: gzip -c web/dist/main.*.js | wc -c.
- [ ] Record current web/dist/main.*.css gzipped size.
- [ ] Note React + react-markdown + remark-gfm + rehype-* are already in there.

### Post-migration targets
- [ ] A la carte path: expect +30 kB gz (4 Radix packages + cmdk). Anything more deserves scrutiny.
- [ ] Full migration: expect +80-120 kB gz before Tailwind purge; purged Tailwind CSS should be smaller than our ~2,900-line styles.css. Net could be neutral.

### Re-measure after each child lands
- [ ] sync-ea8 lands -> re-measure, compare.
- [ ] sync-kmt lands -> re-measure.
- [ ] sync-30s lands -> re-measure (Tailwind output replaces parts of styles.css).

## Complex pieces
- Tooling: axe-core can run headlessly via Playwright; we don't have Playwright wired. Cheapest route: Lighthouse via Chrome DevTools manually.
- gzip vs brotli: brotli is closer to production but gzip is the standard comparison metric.

## Estimate
3-4 h for the initial audit; ~30 min recurring after each child task lands.

title: Migration matrix: which components swap, which stay custom
description:
## Why
Before any execution, agree on the per-component verdict. Some components have direct shadcn equivalents (and would benefit). Others are domain-specific (Timeline, Poll, MessageRow, ThreadPane) and have no shadcn equivalent — migration touches only their **inner primitives** (Button, Avatar, Tooltip), not their structure.

This task is a **planning artifact**, not implementation. Output is a table + a recommendation per component.

## Components to classify

| Current component | Action | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| \`Sidebar\` | KEEP — restyle only | Layout is bespoke (brand + search + GROUPS + DMs + footer). Inner \`Button\` for + actions could swap. |
| \`RoomHeader\` | KEEP — Tabs swap to shadcn | Outer layout custom; tab strip → shadcn \`Tabs\`. |
| \`ChatView\` | KEEP | Domain-specific message list + composer wrapper. |
| \`MessageRow\` | KEEP | Bubble, author chip, mentions, thread badge, poll, reactions — all custom. |
| \`Composer\` | KEEP — popover swaps | Replace mention popup with Radix Popover + cmdk (sync-kmt). |
| \`PollWidget\` | KEEP | No shadcn equivalent. |
| \`ThreadPane\` | KEEP | Custom layout (header + parent + divider + replies + composer). |
| \`TimelineRail\` | KEEP — tooltip swaps | Vertical rail is custom; per-node tooltip → Radix Tooltip. |
| \`AgentRoster\` | KEEP | Custom grouped list with status sections. |
| \`ContextMenu\` | REPLACE | → Radix \`ContextMenu\` (sync-ea8). |
| \`ScrollControls\` | KEEP | Tiny custom thing; not worth the shadcn-Button swap. |
| \`Avatar\` (primitives) | OPTIONAL swap | shadcn \`Avatar\` exists but we don't use the fallback-image flow. Square 5px radius + identity color is custom — keep our impl. |
| \`MentionChip\` (primitives) | KEEP | Custom badge. |
| \`StatusDot\` (primitives) | KEEP | One \`<span>\`, no benefit to abstracting. |
| \`Sticker\` (primitives) | KEEP | No shadcn equivalent. |
| \`Markdown\` | KEEP | Wraps react-markdown — orthogonal to shadcn. |

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Table finalized and committed to \`web/DESIGN.md\` as an appendix.
- [ ] Each "REPLACE" entry has a child issue (sync-ea8, sync-kmt, etc.).
- [ ] Each "KEEP — X swaps" entry has the inner-primitive swap scoped.
- [ ] Discussion of bundle delta: roughly which Radix packages we'd pull in (\`react-context-menu\`, \`react-popover\`, \`react-tooltip\`, \`react-tabs\` — 4 packages, ~30 kB gzipped) for à la carte; more for full migration.

## Output
A short ADR or DESIGN.md section, not code.

## Estimate
2 h.

title: Integrate Tailwind CSS into the Bun.build pipeline
description:
## Why
Tailwind is a precondition for any shadcn adoption. Bun.build (\`web/build.ts\`) is currently a single \`Bun.build({...})\` call with no PostCSS / asset preprocessing step. Tailwind needs either PostCSS or its own v4 CLI run before bundling.

## Approach options
1. **Tailwind v4 CLI sidecar.** Run \`tailwindcss -i web/src/tailwind.css -o web/dist/tw.css\` as a step in \`build.ts\` before/alongside \`Bun.build\`. Simpler — no PostCSS plugin chain. Tailwind v4 (released 2024) is the first version that does this cleanly.
2. **PostCSS wrapper around Bun.build.** Heavier — requires postcss-loader-equivalent for Bun, which is not first-class today.

**Recommendation:** option 1.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`bun web/build.ts\` produces a Tailwind-built CSS file alongside the existing bundle.
- [ ] Existing \`styles.css\` + \`extra.css\` still load (we ship both during the transition).
- [ ] Tailwind config (\`tailwind.config.ts\` or v4 \`@theme\` block) mirrors \`DESIGN.md\` tokens: colors (paper / paper-2 / paper-3 / ink / ink-soft / ink-faint / yellow / pink / blue / lime / tangerine / lilac / teal / red / muted), the 0/3/5/8/999 radius ramp, the shadow scale (\`shadow-sm: 2px 2px 0 var(--rule)\`, etc.).
- [ ] Dark theme works via Tailwind's \`dark:\` variant + \`data-theme="dark"\` selector.
- [ ] Build time stays under ~2 seconds.
- [ ] No visual regression — light + dark screenshots match the current preview.

## Complex pieces
- **Bun.build doesn't watch CSS by default.** Need to wire Tailwind's \`--watch\` into our dev loop.
- **CSS variable interop.** DESIGN.md tokens are already CSS vars; Tailwind config should reference them (\`backgroundColor: { paper: 'var(--paper)' }\`) so we don't fork the source of truth.
- **Bundle delivery.** The current \`<link rel="stylesheet">\` is rewritten by \`build.ts\` to an absolute \`/web/\` path. Tailwind output needs the same treatment.

## Estimate
2–4 h.
notes:
WONTFIX — 2026-05-23

This task is not being pursued. Rationale, from the 2026-05-23 web UI design discussion (see sync-99s decision note for the full context):

The user's goal is a codebase flexible enough to support multiple aesthetics later (neo-brutalist today; vanilla-Slack-style minimal as a possible future theme), togglable from one button. Two paths were considered:

  A. À la carte Radix adoption — replace only accessibility-critical homegrown widgets (ContextMenu, Toast, mention popup) with Radix headless primitives. Keep the existing custom-CSS + CSS-custom-property token system. Multi-theme support comes from completing the tokenization work in sync-75p.

  B. Full shadcn migration — add Tailwind, port ~93 KB of custom CSS to Tailwind utility classes, re-theme every shadcn primitive to brutalist.

Audit findings (2026-05-23) showed the codebase is already ~70% of the way to token-driven multi-theme support: components use semantic class names (182 className= sites, all semantic), aesthetic colors are not hardcoded in components, raw visual literals are concentrated in styles.css/extra.css where they are mechanically swappable to tokens. The Tailwind precondition this bead implements adds significant migration cost (months of CSS rewrite) for a benefit (theme tokens) that the existing system delivers more cheaply via sync-75p.

Decision: pursue Path A. This bead (Tailwind integration into Bun.build) becomes unnecessary. Closing as wontfix to declutter `bd ready`. If full shadcn is ever reconsidered, this bead's approach notes (Tailwind v4 CLI sidecar vs PostCSS wrapper) remain valid and can be referenced from the closed-issue history.
close_reason: Not pursuing per 2026-05-23 design decision recorded in sync-99s. Chose à la carte Radix (no Tailwind, no full shadcn migration). Rationale captured in each bead's WONTFIX note.

title: Evaluate/adopt shadcn for web chat UI
description:
## Goal
Decide between two paths and execute the chosen one:
(A) **À la carte Radix adoption** — replace only the accessibility-critical homegrown components (ContextMenu, mention popover, future ⌘K palette) with headless Radix primitives. Keep the rest of the CSS / visual system intact. ~6-10h.
(B) **Full shadcn migration** — add Tailwind + shadcn CLI, port the ~2,900-line CSS to Tailwind tokens + utility classes, re-theme every shadcn primitive to brutalist, replace homegrown primitives with shadcn equivalents where they exist. ~4-7 focused days.

## Current state (as of this issue)
- React 19 + TypeScript + Bun.build pipeline (no PostCSS / no Vite).
- Plain CSS — \`web/src/styles.css\` (~2,500 lines) + \`web/src/components/extra.css\` (~900 lines).
- Custom primitives: \`Avatar\`, \`Sticker\`, \`StatusDot\`, \`MentionChip\`, \`CountChip\`.
- Custom interactive components with no a11y baseline: \`ContextMenu\`, mention popup in \`Composer\`, \`ScrollControls\`, \`TimelineRail\` tooltip.
- Brutalist visual system formalized in \`web/DESIGN.md\` (tokens for paper/ink/accents, 0/3/5/8/999 radius ramp, \`--shadow\` / \`--shadow-sm\` / \`--shadow-lg\` offset scale, agent identity colors).
- DataSource abstraction (\`MockDataSource\` + future \`DaemonDataSource\`) — **not affected by either path**.

## Why this matters
1. **Accessibility debt**. Our \`ContextMenu\` has no keyboard nav, no focus trap, no \`aria-haspopup\` wiring. Same for the mention popover. Radix gives all of that for free.
2. **Future surfaces become cheap**. ⌘K palette, Toast, Dialog, Drawer, Sheet — these are easier once Radix (or shadcn) is in.
3. **Tailwind-vs-custom-CSS** is a separate axis. Tailwind unlocks shadcn's \`cva\` variant API and \`dark:\` variant, but means migrating ~2,900 lines of CSS.

## Decision points (need answers before executing)
- [ ] Accept Tailwind in the build pipeline? (Bun.build has no PostCSS — would run Tailwind v4 CLI as a sidecar in \`build.ts\`.)
- [ ] Keep the brutalist identity exactly as it is, or accept some drift toward shadcn's defaults? (shadcn.io has a "Neo Brutalism" preset that gets ~70% of the way.)
- [ ] Bundle size budget — Radix primitives are 5-15 kB gzip each; ~8-10 of them is +80-100 kB. Tailwind purged is small. Acceptable?
- [ ] Timeline pressure — accessibility wins are urgent, full migration is not.

## Recommended path
**Adopt à la carte (sync-EPIC.A) first**, ship the accessibility wins in a week. Then decide on full migration based on whether other shadcn primitives (Dialog, Sheet, Toast, Command) start showing up in feature requests.

## Children (see linked issues)
- A. Tailwind + Bun.build pipeline integration
- B. Radix ContextMenu replacement (à la carte path)
- C. Radix Popover for mention autocomplete (à la carte path)
- D. Brutalist re-theming of shadcn primitives (full-migration path)
- E. Component-by-component migration matrix (planning artifact)
- F. Bundle-size + a11y audit baseline (so we can measure the wins)
notes:
DECISION (2026-05-23, recorded after design discussion):

Path chosen: **À la carte Radix adoption** (Option A in the original epic). Not full shadcn, not Tailwind.

Resolved decision points:
- Tailwind in the build pipeline: NO. The custom-CSS + custom-prop token system is already 70% of the way to multi-theme readiness (see sync-75p audit). Tailwind would be a months-long rewrite of ~93 KB of CSS for a benefit we mostly already have.
- Brutalist identity: KEEP. Radix is headless — we render with existing className skin layer. Visual diff target = zero on all migrated widgets.
- Bundle size budget: ACCEPTABLE. Three Radix deps (~30-45 kB gzip combined), tree-shakeable.
- Timeline: deferred behind sync-75p (tokenization). Rationale — re-skinning Radix widgets per future theme is then a pure CSS exercise, not a tangled visual+behavioral diff.

Implication for existing children:
- sync-ea8 (ContextMenu): proceeds as planned. PROOF POINT — if Radix portals don't respect [data-theme=...] cascade, the rest is reconsidered.
- sync-kmt (mention popup): proceeds as planned, after sync-ea8 validates approach.
- sync-30s (Tailwind integration): NOT PURSUED per this decision. Candidate for close-as-wontfix.
- sync-157 (full-migration re-theme): NOT PURSUED per this decision. Candidate for close-as-wontfix.
- sync-e0t (migration matrix): still useful as a record; complete it post-Radix-slice or close as superseded.
- sync-0ap (baseline measurements): nice-to-have; if skipped, document why in the verification bead.

Two new children added under this epic today:
- Toast Radix migration (missing widget).
- Final slice verification (cross-cutting regression check).

Dependency added: sync-99s depends-on sync-75p — Radix work waits until tokenization lands. This ordering is intentional; see sync-75p design notes.

title: P0: Codex MCP notifications are log-visible but not model-visible
description:
## Summary
Codex sessions registered through `synchronize` do not appear to receive model-visible or user-visible real-time notifications from standard MCP `notifications/message`, even though the synchronize MCP poller fetches the directed events and sends the notification successfully. This is now the next major issue to solve for making Codex feel as live as Claude channel sessions.

## Live Reproduction Context
Runtime was intentionally reused at `~/.synchronize` with daemon `http://127.0.0.1:60756`, pid `8920`.

Peers at the time of investigation:
- Bob: Claude peer `7e135d62-147d-4114-8ca7-fc58742e042d`
- Alice: Claude peer `70fbc691-9101-4289-a070-63e266890030`
- Jerry: Codex peer `3dc7fa58-a557-4c6f-8d87-451d4b8884a2`, `tool=codex`, session name `Jerry`

Observed sequence:
1. Jerry registered through the Codex synchronize MCP.
2. Jerry DM'd Bob and Alice.
3. Alice replied to Jerry.
4. Bob replied to Jerry.
5. Alice later sent another DM to Jerry: `Hey Jerry, what's your session/peer ID?`
6. Jerry did not appear to receive a visible prompt/notification in the Codex UI despite the MCP poller running.

## Evidence Captured
SQLite state showed Jerry's peer existed and was online by lease. Jerry's `last_cursor` advanced to event `12`, proving the Codex polling path consumed through the latest event.

Relevant events:
- event `10`: Alice -> Jerry, delivered at `2026-05-10T12:08:49.558Z`, later read/acked by Jerry's `bridge_inbox` call.
- event `11`: Bob -> Jerry, delivered at `2026-05-10T12:08:52.064Z`, later read/acked by Jerry's `bridge_inbox` call.
- event `12`: Alice -> Jerry, delivered at `2026-05-10T12:09:52.641Z`, still unread/unacked at investigation time.

Codex logs showed:
- `bridge_register` for Jerry succeeded with `notify_mode=codex`.
- `synchronize-mcp` started `Codex polling notifier active peer_id=3dc7fa58-a557-4c6f-8d87-451d4b8884a2`.
- The notifier received event IDs `10`, `11`, and `12`.
- The MCP server sent Codex logging notifications for those event IDs.
- Codex accepted them as `MCP server log message` records.

This proves daemon write, durable inbox, `/events/{peer_id}` polling, cursor advancement, and MCP `notifications/message` emission all worked. The failure is that Codex did not turn the MCP logging notification into a model-visible interruption/prompt.

## Reference Investigation
User pointed to `https://gitlab.com/Dave3991/peers-mcp` as the intended reference, not the local `claude-peers-mcp` project.

Sub-agent Dewey inspected:
- `peers-mcp-plan.md`
- `src/Peers.Mcp/Program.cs`
- `src/Peers.Mcp/Lifecycle/ChannelNotifier.cs`
- `src/Peers.Mcp/Tools/PollTools.cs`
- broker poll/inbox code

Key findings:
- `peers-mcp` also uses standard MCP `notifications/message` for Codex push.
- `peers-mcp` treats that push as best-effort, not as guaranteed model-visible delivery.
- `peers-mcp` makes durable inbox polling (`peer_inbox`) the primary agent-visible delivery workflow for directed messages.
- Its instructions tell agents to call inbox at startup, after task chunks, on user request, and before finishing.
- It has a `peer_test_push` diagnostic tool that explicitly says: if push does not surface, use inbox fallback.
- Its `peer_inbox` has at-least-once semantics by deferring ack until the next inbox call.

## Current synchronize Behavior
Current `synchronize` Codex path:
- `bridge_register` starts `NotificationBridge`.
- `NotificationBridge` polls `GET /events/{peer_id}` with a bounded buffer.
- On new events it calls `emitMcpNotification(..., mode='codex', event)`.
- Codex path uses `sendLoggingMessage`, producing standard MCP `notifications/message`.

This is architecturally similar to `peers-mcp`, but the docs and skill wording overstate Codex notification behavior.

Current gaps:
- README says Codex gets standard MCP `notifications/message`, but does not clearly state that Codex may only log it and may not surface it to the model.
- MCP server instructions are Claude-channel-centric and say channel messages should be responded to immediately; this is not applicable to Codex.
- `skills/synchronize-codex/SKILL.md` says `bridge_inbox` is a fallback, while for Codex it should be the primary reliable delivery path.
- `bridge_inbox` immediately acks returned events when `ack: true` is passed. A peers-mcp-style deferred ack would be safer for at-least-once processing.
- Codex logging notification payload is the raw event object, not a concise human-readable message.
- The notifier cursor currently starts from `0`; consider starting from registered peer state / `last_cursor` to avoid old-event replay on session startup.

## Desired Outcome
Make Codex delivery semantics explicit, robust, and testable.

Possible implementation direction:
- Keep the existing one-peer Codex polling notifier. This preserves the performance constraint and architecture.
- Treat MCP `notifications/message` as best-effort diagnostic/log push, not guaranteed prompt delivery.
- Make `bridge_inbox` the primary reliable Codex delivery surface.
- Update Codex skill instructions so agents call `bridge_inbox` after registration, after meaningful task chunks, when the user asks about peer activity, and before final response.
- Consider changing `bridge_inbox` to peers-mcp-style deferred ack: ack previously returned IDs on the next call, then fetch current unacked rows.
- Consider adding `bridge_test_push` to verify whether a client surfaces MCP push or only logs it.
- Format Codex logging notifications as concise text plus metadata for better log visibility.
- Add explicit troubleshooting docs for the Jerry/Alice case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Codex behavior is accurately documented: MCP `notifications/message` is best-effort/log-visible; `bridge_inbox` is the reliable model-visible path.
- `skills/synchronize-codex/SKILL.md` requires routine `bridge_inbox` checks, matching the peers-mcp workflow.
- MCP server instructions distinguish Claude channel behavior from Codex inbox behavior.
- Tests cover that Codex notification emission does not remove durable inbox messages.
- Tests cover deferred or otherwise safe inbox ack semantics if implemented.
- Tests cover Codex notification formatting if changed.
- README troubleshooting includes the Jerry/Alice reproduction and explains why the daemon can show delivered while Codex still does not visibly prompt.
- No changes introduce per-group polling, WebSocket/SSE, cloud sync, or any violation of the original synchronize constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Codex delivery contract is corrected and implemented: notifications/message is best-effort/log-visible, bridge_inbox is the reliable model-visible path, docs/skills/tests capture the Jerry/Alice reproduction and peers-mcp comparison.

title: Re-theme shadcn primitives to brutalist (full-migration path)
description:
## Why
This task only fires if we choose path (B) — full shadcn migration. shadcn primitives ship with a minimal-modern default look that is the **opposite** of our brutalist identity (soft shadows, thin borders, muted palette). Adopting shadcn means re-theming every component we use.

## Scope of primitives that need re-theming
| shadcn primitive | We'd use it as | Brutalist tweaks needed |
|---|---|---|
| \`Button\` | \`SEND\` button, \`+\` new-group, all icon buttons | Ink fill, paper text, hard offset \`--shadow-sm\`, \`translate(-1px,-1px)\` on hover, no Tailwind \`hover:bg-opacity\` softness |
| \`Avatar\` | Replace \`primitives.tsx#Avatar\` | Square 5px radius, ink border 2.5px, hard offset shadow, agent identity color fill, \`inkFor(color)\` text |
| \`Tabs\` | \`CHAT / BOARD / ARTIFACTS\` | Active tab gets ink fill + paper text; inactive gets transparent + ink-soft text; underline indicator is solid 3px, no soft transition |
| \`Tooltip\` | TimelineRail event hover | Paper bg, ink border, hard offset \`--shadow\`, no fade/scale animation (we use a custom 140ms tooltip-in keyframe) |
| \`Popover\` | Mention popup (see sync-kmt) | Same as Tooltip styling |
| \`ContextMenu\` | Right-click on 4 surfaces (see sync-ea8) | \`.ctx-menu\` styling preserved verbatim |
| \`ScrollArea\` | Replace our hand-rolled \`autoscroll\` | Match the auto-hide thumb behavior + per-surface \`--scroll-bg\` tinting we already have |
| \`Dialog\` / \`Sheet\` | Future ⌘K, settings panel, "new room" modal | Brutalist border + shadow; no backdrop blur (DESIGN.md forbids blur) |
| \`Toast\` | Future notification surface | Same |
| \`Separator\` | Section dividers in sidebar, thread divider | Dashed or solid 1.5px \`--rule\` |

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] shadcn's "Neo Brutalism" preset is installed as a starting point (cuts ~70% of the work).
- [ ] Theme overrides written as a single \`web/src/components/ui/theme.css\` or via Tailwind \`@layer components\` so each primitive has a clearly named override block.
- [ ] Light + dark theme visual parity with the current preview (screenshot diff in PR).
- [ ] The brutalist character (hard offset shadows, ink borders, agent identity colors) survives all primitive swaps.

## Complex pieces
- **Variant system.** shadcn uses \`cva\` for variants (e.g. \`<Button variant="destructive" size="sm">\`). We'd need to author brutalist variants — \`accent="yellow"\`, \`accent="pink"\`, etc. — to keep agent identity colors flowing through.
- **\`Sticker\` primitive has no shadcn equivalent.** Keep as-is.
- **The agent color → contrast text mapping (\`inkFor()\`)** isn't expressible in Tailwind utility classes alone — it needs to live in JS (passed via \`style\`) or as a per-agent CSS class with a hardcoded color.
- **Dark mode.** shadcn assumes \`.dark\` class on \`<html>\`; we use \`[data-theme="dark"]\`. Configurable via Tailwind config but needs the right selector.
- **No-blur rule.** DESIGN.md explicitly forbids \`backdrop-filter: blur()\`. Many shadcn primitives (Dialog overlay, Tooltip in some presets) default to a blurred backdrop. Strip those.

## Estimate
1–2 days, assuming Tailwind is already integrated (sync-30s) and the Neo Brutalism preset is the starting point.
notes:
WONTFIX — 2026-05-23

This task is not being pursued. Rationale (full context in sync-99s decision note + the wontfix note on sync-30s):

This bead was scoped to the full-shadcn migration path (Path B in the 2026-05-23 evaluation). The chosen path is Path A — à la carte Radix headless primitives only, with no Tailwind and no shadcn primitives. Therefore there are no shadcn primitives to re-theme.

The Radix slice (sync-ea8 ContextMenu, sync-kmt mention popup, sync-99s.1 Toast) does NOT require this re-theming work: Radix ships headless behavior with no default visual styling, so we render Radix components with the existing .ctx-menu / .toast / .mention-pop className skin layer untouched. Visual diff target on each Radix migration is zero.

Closing as wontfix. The detailed brutalist tweak table in this bead (Button, Avatar, Tabs, Tooltip styling rules) is still useful reference material if the full-shadcn path is ever revived; it remains accessible via the closed-issue history.
close_reason: Not pursuing per 2026-05-23 design decision recorded in sync-99s. Chose à la carte Radix (no Tailwind, no full shadcn migration). Rationale captured in each bead's WONTFIX note.
